body_txt: Naming a work after its protagonist is one of the most popular title conventions in fiction, to the point that when a title isn't eponymous to the protagonist, everybody will still assume it is . Often a One-Word Title , although there are examples with more than one word, such as Adjective Noun Fred if Fred is the protagonist. If the Protagonist Title is also a pun, it's an Epunymous Title . If the title also includes the protagonist's occupation, it's Role Called . See also Character Name and the Noun Phrase , The Full Name Adventures , Mononymous Biopic Title , Multi-Character Title , Name and Name , Protagonist and Friends , Secondary Character Title . Compare to Antagonist Title , contrast with Secondary Character Title . Subtrope of Character Title . Adjective Noun Fred usually overlaps with this, having the "Fred" be the protagonist.
